name: Beetlejuice
description: Tim Burton's 1988 gothic comedy — say his name three times… Beetlejuice, Beetlejuice, Beetlejuice.

questions.q: Who directed the 1988 film Beetlejuice?

source.notes: Burton's breakout following Pee-wee's Big Adventure.

questions.q: Who plays the title character, the 'ghost with the most'?

source.notes: Keaton later reunited with Burton on Batman.

questions.q: What must you do to summon Beetlejuice?
choices: Knock three times on his grave

source.original_a: By uttering his name three times
source.notes: Classic rule of thirds in the film.

questions.q: Which teen actress plays goth outsider Lydia Deetz?

source.notes: Ryder was 16 during production.

questions.q: What are the first names of the deceased couple whose house the Deetzes move into?

source.notes: Played by Alec Baldwin and Geena Davis.

questions.q: Which calypso song do the Deetzes hilariously get possessed into dancing to at the dinner table?

source.original_a: Harry Belafonte's Calypso and Jump Up Calypso
source.notes: Harry Belafonte's 'Day-O.'

questions.q: The Maitlands discover a book titled what, meant to guide the newly deceased?

source.notes: A running gag throughout the film.

questions.q: What is the name of Lydia Deetz's sculptor stepmother?

source.notes: Played by Catherine O'Hara.

questions.q: Who plays Charles Deetz, Lydia's burned-out real-estate-developer father?

source.notes: Jones also starred in Amadeus and Ferris Bueller's Day Off.

questions.q: Beetlejuice is played for laughs as an unlicensed 'what' in the underworld?

source.notes: Joke on traditional exorcism.

questions.q: How do Adam and Barbara Maitland die at the start of the film?
choices: A car crashes off a covered bridge

source.original_a: Their car plunges off a bridge into the river
source.notes: Swerving to avoid a dog.

questions.q: In the waiting room of the afterlife, workers have shriveled bodies caused by which specific cause of death?
choices: Suicide — they work as civil servants forever

source.notes: Juno explains the afterlife job assignment.

questions.q: Adam's elaborate basement hobby is building what?
choices: A scale model of the town

source.original_a: Building a scale model of the town
source.notes: Becomes critical to Beetlejuice's plot.

questions.q: Which massive creature saves the day at the film's climax by eating Beetlejuice?

source.notes: Sandworms inhabit the 'Outside' Titan/afterlife Saturn.

questions.q: At the end of the film, Beetlejuice tries to force what kind of union to escape the afterlife?

source.notes: Climactic wedding scene.

questions.q: Which red-headed sprite visits the Maitlands as their afterlife caseworker, Juno, in a puff of smoke?

source.notes: Sylvia Sidney as Juno; smoke literally leaves her mouth through her slit throat.

questions.q: Which composer scored Beetlejuice, starting a long collaboration with Tim Burton?

source.notes: Elfman, former Oingo Boingo frontman, scored most of Burton's films.

questions.q: What is Beetlejuice's costume color scheme?
choices: Black and white stripes with flecks of rot

source.notes: Iconic moldy pinstripe suit.

questions.q: What was the working title the studio originally preferred before Tim Burton insisted on 'Beetlejuice'?

source.notes: Burton reportedly joked he'd walk if they called it 'Scared Sheetless.'

questions.q: Beetlejuice won an Academy Award in 1989 in which category?

source.notes: Won for Best Makeup.

questions.q: Tim Burton released a long-awaited sequel in 2024 starring most of the original cast. It was titled what?

source.notes: Released September 6, 2024.

questions.q: Who plays the shady afterlife 'paranormal expert' Otho, hired to exorcise the Maitlands?

source.notes: Shadix also voiced the Mayor in The Nightmare Before Christmas.
